notable_pick_trades: Acquired 6th-round picks from SF (Keion White trade) and PIT (Kyle Dugger trade) during 2025 season. Extra 4th-round pick via CHI/KC. Four 6th-round picks total.
roster_context: Coming off a stunning 14-3 turnaround season under Mike Vrabel that ended with a Super Bowl LX loss to Seattle (29-13). Drake Maye established himself as a franchise QB in Year 2. Strong young core but the roster has clear holes at EDGE (pass rush ranked 26th in sacks), OL (playoff struggles), and WR (releasing Stefon Diggs). $41.3M in cap space with only 14 pending free agents. Free agency opens March 11 — this assessment is pre-free agency but incorporates known moves through March 5.

needs.explanation: The Patriots' biggest need. They ranked 26th in sacks (35) and 20th in sack percentage in 2025. K'Lavon Chaisson (7.5 sacks) is a UFA who may price himself out. Harold Landry III (8.5 sacks, team leader) dealt with a knee injury from Week 6 onward, missed the AFC Championship, and hinted at offseason surgery — he turns 30 and his $11M salary is guaranteed. Anfernee Jennings has limited pass-rush upside (4.0 sacks). Elijah Ponder (4 sacks) was a pleasant surprise as a UDFA but is unproven long-term. EVP Eliot Wolf publicly highlighted the edge rusher class at the Combine, calling it 'obviously an area of need.' Vrabel emphasized wanting 'violence' and 'disruption' at the position.
needs.current_starter: Harold Landry III (under contract, coming off knee injury), K'Lavon Chaisson (UFA)
needs.depth: Anfernee Jennings (signed through 2027, limited pass rusher), Elijah Ponder (UDFA breakout), Bradyn Swinson (5th-round rookie, 27 snaps)

needs.explanation: Khyiris Tonga (3yr/$21M, Chiefs) was the Patriots' top nose tackle and a critical early-down run defender. Cory Durden is the next man up but is a lesser player. Milton Williams and Christian Barmore (charges dropped) are interior options but New England lacks a true run-stuffing nose. Need escalates from depth concern to a genuine starter vacancy. Interior DL should be a Day 2 draft target.

Left guard was a problem in 2025. Jared Wilson (2025 3rd-round pick) started at LG but is a natural center who struggled at times at guard. Multiple analysts noted Wilson is 'not an NFL-caliber left guard' and the team needs a veteran upgrade beside Campbell. The Patriots' plan appears to be moving Wilson to center (his college position at Georgia) and finding a starting LG, either in free agency or the draft. Garrett Bradbury at C is adequate but not elite (29 pass-block losses in 2024 with MIN). The interior OL could use an upgrade at guard to better protect Maye.
needs.current_starter: Jared Wilson (LG, better suited at C), Garrett Bradbury (C), Mike Onwenu (RG)
needs.depth: Caedan Wallace (versatile), Ben Brown, Brenden Jaimes, Mekhi Butler
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4 (prefer veteran FA signing at LG)

needs.explanation: Morgan Moses turned 35 and is entering the final stretch of his career. He started all 21 games in 2025 and was solid, but the Patriots need a long-term successor at RT. Will Campbell (2025 1st-round pick, LT) had a strong regular season but struggled in the playoffs after a knee injury in November. Backup OTs Vederian Lowe and Thayer Munford are both UFAs. Marcus Bryant (7th-round pick) is a developmental project. The OL was overwhelmed in the Super Bowl (Maye sacked 6 times) and Wolf acknowledged they 'didn't finish the way we would have hoped.' An early-round heir to Moses at RT who can sit for a year is a smart investment.
needs.current_starter: Will Campbell (LT, under contract), Morgan Moses (RT, under contract, age 35)
needs.depth: Caedan Wallace, Marcus Bryant (7th-round developmental), Vederian Lowe (UFA), Thayer Munford (UFA)

needs.explanation: Stefon Diggs will be released when the league year opens March 11, creating a massive hole at WR1. Diggs was the coverage-dictating receiver the offense needed (ranked 2nd in ESPN receiver score, had 1,000-yard season). Behind Diggs, the corps includes Mack Hollins (solid but limited WR2), DeMario Douglas (slot, ranked 11th in receiver score), Kayshon Boutte (25th), and Kyle Williams (rookie with field-stretching ability but still developing). The receiving grade dropped from 87.6 in the regular season to 63.2 in the playoffs. The Patriots have shown interest in Alec Pierce (FA) and a potential A.J. Brown or D.J. Moore trade, but they still need to add a true WR1 caliber player via draft or acquisition.
needs.current_starter: Mack Hollins, DeMario Douglas, Kayshon Boutte
needs.depth: Kyle Williams (2025 3rd-round pick, developing), Efton Chism III
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 if not addressed in FA/trade

needs.explanation: Wolf stated at the Combine that New England wants to add youth at inside linebacker behind veterans Robert Spillane, Jahlani Tavai, and Christian Elliss. Jack Gibbens (RFA) is a re-sign candidate but not a certainty. The Patriots' LB corps is experienced but aging and lacks long-term developmental talent. Spillane is the anchor but the defense needs a young off-ball LB who can eventually step into a starting role.
needs.current_starter: Robert Spillane, Jahlani Tavai, Christian Elliss
needs.depth: Jack Gibbens (RFA), Marte Mapu, Chad Muma

needs.explanation: Antonio Gibson was released ($2.14M savings), removing the team's primary kick returner and change-of-pace back. Rhamondre Stevenson and TreVeyon Henderson (outstanding 2025 rookie, 911 rushing yards) form a strong top two. Depth is thin but not critical — Terrell Jennings and Lan Larison are roster depth pieces. A late-round or UDFA addition could fill the returner void.
needs.depth: Terrell Jennings, Lan Larison, Elijah Mitchell (futures contract)

needs.explanation: Jaylinn Hawkins is a UFA after a career season (4 INTs, 1.5 sacks, 91.3% of defensive snaps). Craig Woodson (2025 4th-round pick) emerged as a building-block starter at FS, but if Hawkins leaves, the Patriots need a replacement starting SS. Dell Pettus is the only other safety under contract with any game experience. The team is taking a 'patient approach' with Hawkins and he reportedly hopes to 'remain home.' If re-signed, this drops to tier 4. If not, it becomes tier 2.
needs.current_starter: Craig Woodson (FS, under contract), Jaylinn Hawkins (SS, UFA)
needs.depth: Dell Pettus, Brenden Schooler (ST specialist), John Saunders Jr.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3-5 for depth even if Hawkins returns

needs.explanation: Hunter Henry is entering his age-31 season and is the only TE on the roster with significant experience. Austin Hooper is a UFA. The team needs to find a long-term complement or eventual successor to Henry. The Combine scouts were looking at athletic TEs like Roush. Jack Westover (ERFA) and C.J. Dippre are unproven behind Henry. Kyle Pitts (franchise tagged by ATL) was a Patriots target that is now unavailable.
needs.depth: C.J. Ham, Jack Westover (ERFA) — very thin behind Henry

needs.explanation: Christian Gonzalez (extension imminent, per reporting) and Carlton Davis III anchor the CB room. Marcus Jones is the nickel/CB3 under contract. Alex Austin not tendered but was only CB5. CB is not a need — no adjustment required.

Khyiris Tonga is a UFA after a breakout 2025 season as a nose tackle. If he departs, the Patriots lose their run-stuffing NT. However, the interior DL is still in good shape with Milton Williams and Christian Barmore as an elite tandem, plus Cory Durden, Leonard Taylor III, and 2025 4th-rounder Joshua Farmer showing promise. Extension talks with Tonga fell apart during the season. A late-round NT to replace Tonga's role would be prudent.
needs.depth: Cory Durden, Leonard Taylor III, Joshua Farmer, Eric Gregory (IR)

free_agency_impact: Signed Romeo Doubs (WR) from Green Bay Packers — $17.0M AAV
Signed Alijah Vera-Tucker (IOL) from New York Jets — $14.0M AAV
Signed Dre'Mont Jones (EDGE) from Baltimore Ravens — $12.2M AAV
Signed Kevin Byard (S) from Chicago Bears — $9.0M AAV
Signed Reggie Gilliam (RB) from Buffalo Bills — $4.0M AAV
Signed Julian Hill (TE) from Miami Dolphins — terms TBD AAV
Signed Mike Brown (S) from Tennessee Titans — terms TBD AAV
Signed K.J. Britt (LB) from Miami Dolphins — terms TBD AAV
Signed Jesse Luketa (EDGE) from Arizona Cardinals — $1.1M AAV
Signed Tommy DeVito (QB) from New England Patriots — $2.2M AAV
scheme_fit_notes: Under Vrabel and new DC Zak Kuhr, the Patriots run an aggressive 4-down front defense. Edge rushers need to be violent, explosive first-step players who can set the edge in the run game AND rush the passer — Vrabel explicitly values 'violence' and 'disruption.' Interior DL features penetrating 3-techs (Williams, Barmore) paired with space-eating NTs. Off-ball LBs must be versatile enough to blitz from interior or drop into coverage. On offense, Josh McDaniels' system values centers who are assignment-sound and move well in space, and wide receivers who can separate and win contested catches. The Patriots prefer high-character, competitive players who fit their 'Do Your Job' culture.
